data.title: SpringWorks Therapeutics jumps amid takeover speculation
data.description:
• SpringWorks Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SWTX) advanced 6.3% amid takeover speculation.
  • The biotech firm is said to be at the center of renewed takeover speculation, according to a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Friday.
  • Last February, Betaville reported that SpringWorks (NASDAQ:SWTX) was said to be a potential target. At the time, a Swiss pharma firm and Abbvie (ABBV) were said to be looking at SpringWorks.
  • It's not clear which company is currently looking at SpringWorks (SWTX), according to the alert.
  • SpringWorks (SWTX) is set to present at the annual JPMorgan healthcare conference on Monday.

data.title: Quantum stocks slide after D-Wave CEO refutes Nvidia's Huang
data.description:
Quantum stocks started falling during afternoon trading on Friday, despite an uptick earlier in the day.

Quantum Computing (NASDAQ:QUBT) shares were down 7%, Rigetti (NASDAQ:RGTI) -4.5%, Aqrqit Quantum (NASDAQ:ARQQ) -9% and D-Wave Quantum (NYSE:QBTS) -1%. However, IonQ (NYSE:IONQ) proved to be an outlier as it had jumped 7%.

Early Friday, IonQ's Chairman and CEO Peter Chapman said he expects bookings for the quantum computing company to be at the "high end" of its forecast, and forecast revenue could approach $1B by 2030.

D-Wave shares were up about 3% during pre-market trading Friday after the firm said its fiscal year 2024 bookings will likely top $23M, representing growth of approximately 120% over fiscal year 2023 bookings.

This followed D-Wave CEO Alan Baratz' refutation of a statement made by Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang during the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas on Wednesday.

"If you kind of said 15 years for very useful quantum computers, that would probably be on the early side," Huang said. "If you said 30, it's probably on the late side. If you picked 20, I think a whole bunch of us would believe it."

"There are different approaches to quantum: annealing and gate are the two primary approaches," Baratz said during an interview on CNBC. "And while his [Huang's] comments may not be totally off base for gate model quantum computers, they are 100% off base for annealing quantum computers. When it comes to D-Wave annealing quantum computing, he is dead wrong. We are not 30 years out, we're not 20 years out, we're not 15 years out. We are today. We are supporting businesses today with quantum compute to solve their hard problems."

Baratz released an official statement on the issue on Thursday, further driving his viewpoint that the age of quantum computing has arrived.

"Jensen Huang's comments are just the latest in a string of misinformation on quantum computing unfortunately driving market reaction," he said.

"There is massive potential for AI and quantum to work together in advancing the limitations of today’s classical computing capabilities," Baratz added. "We welcome the opportunity to partner with companies like NVIDIA in exploring what's possible when QPUs and GPUs come together."

Separately, several AI stocks were down Friday as well. SoundHound AI (SOUN) slumped 6% by afternoon trading. Big Bear.ai Holdings (BBAI) slipped 1.6% and C3.ai (AI) slid 2.3%.

data.title: HP Enterprise as it wins server deal with Elon Musk's X: report
data.description:
• HP Enterprise (HPE) was in focus on Friday after Bloomberg reported that the IT company had won a deal worth more than $1B to provide servers for Elon Musk's social network X.
  • Shares rose more than 3% following the report.
  • The deal was reached late last year, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • Competitors Dell Technologies (DELL) and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) were also said to have bid on the deal, the news outlet added.
  • HP Enterprise and X did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Bank earnings, oil updates, powerhouse conferences in retail and healthcare
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - January 13

  • Volatility watch - Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and SEALSQ (LAES) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Kohl's (KSS) and SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) are near the top of the list of stocks with the highest level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include fuboTV (FUBO), Star Fashion Culture (STFS), Accolade (ACCD). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Martin Midstream Partners (MMLP), Apogee Enterprises (APOG), and Smith Douglas Homes (SDHC).
  • All week - The week ahead includes three heavyweight conferences. The ICR retail conferences of the year will include participation from Walmart (WMT), Shopify (SHOP), US Foods (USFD), Denny's (DENN), Planet Fitness (PLNT), Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF), and Macy's (M) to name a few. ICR typically includes a flurry of holiday sales updates and full-year guidance updates that have led to sizable share price swings in the past. The Needham Growth Conference will include prominent participants such as MaxLinear (MXL), Gannett (GCI), Atomera Incorporated (ATOM), BlackSky Technology Inc. (BKSY), and IonQ (IONQ). The healthcare sector will have its eyes on the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference, with dozens of companies due to present.
  • All day - Five Below (FIVE) is expected to issue a sales update.

Tuesday - January 14

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Applied Digital (APLD).
  • 8:30 a.m. The Producer Price Index report will be released by The Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 10:00 a.m. Kansas City Federal Reserve Bank President Jeffrey Schmid will speak on the economic and monetary policy outlook before the Central Exchange.
  • 1:00 p.m. Shareholders of Gatos Silver (GATO) and First Majestic (AG) will vote on the planned merger of the company.
  • 3:00 p.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give opening remarks before the 4th Annual "An Economy That Works for All: Housing Affordability" event.

Wednesday - January 15

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Wells Fargo (WFC), BlackRock (BLK), Citigroup (C), Goldman Sachs (GS), Kinder Morgan (KMI) and Bank of New York Mellon (BK).
  • All day - The FDA action dates arrives for Atara Biotherapeutics' (ATRA) Tabelecleucel (Tab-cel) treatment.
  • Oil watch - The IEA and OPEC will publish their monthly oil reports.
  • 8:30 a.m. The December Consumer Price Index report will be released by The Bureau of Labor Statistics.
  • 10:00 a.m. Minneapolis Federal Reserve Bank President Neel Kashkari will give welcome remarks and participates in a fireside chat before the virtual Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis 2025 Regional Economic Conditions Conference.
  • 11:00 a.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give a keynote talk before the Connecticut Business and Industry Association Economic Summit and Outlook 2025.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.

Thursday - January 16

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Taiwan Semi (TSM), UnitedHealthcare (UNH), Bank of America (BAC), Morgan Stanley (MS), U.S. Bancorp (USB), J.B. Hunt (JBHT), and M&T Bank (MTB).
  • M&A watch - Flowco Holdings (FLOC) is expected to start trading after pricing its IPO. The firm provides production optimization wellhead products and services for the oil & gas exploration industry.
  • All day - RLI Corp (RLI) will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis following the company's two-for-one stock split.
  • 8:30 a.m. The December Retail Sales Report will be released.
  • 8:45 a.m. UnitedHealth Group (UNH) will hold its earnings conference call in management's first appearance in front of analysts since the murder of CEO Brian Thompson.

Friday - January 17

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Regions Financial (RF), Schlumberger Limited (SLB), and Truist Financial (TFC).
  • 8:30 a.m. The December Housing Starts report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau and the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Valero and Par Pacific upgraded, Phillips 66 cut at Piper Sandler in shift of refiner ratings
data.description:
Valero Energy (NYSE:VLO) +1.4% and Par Pacific (PARR) +3% in Friday's trading as Piper Sandler upgrades both stocks to Overweight from Neutral with respective $149 and $25 price targets, reflecting a more constructive view regarding trends as margins move back towards mid-cycle levels.

Valero (NYSE:VLO) is "a low-cost operator with the highest quality portfolio of advantaged assets, provides both most resilience to near-term margin weakness and highest pure-play leverage to a cyclical recovery of the large-cap refiners," whose refining and distribution business is set to flip from headwind to tailwind in 2025, driven by tightening balances and SAF margin accretion, Piper's Ryan Todd writes.

Par Pacific (PARR) offers the "largest upside, by far, to mid-cycle valuation," with support from moderate improvements in WCS differentials, and its non-refining businesses provide underappreciated downside support in the current low margin environment, Todd says, noting that the company generates free cash flow even at current strip prices.

However, Phillips 66 (PSX) -2.6% as Todd downgrades shares to Neutral from Overweight with a $130 price target, citing the company's lowest leverage to cyclical recovery in refining margins of its large-cap peers, and its leverage remains well above the high end of targeted leverage metrics, which likely will limit shareholder returns somewhat in 2025 to lower net debt; recent midstream acquisitions have increased investor uncertainty and risk surrounding capital allocation.

data.title: UFC reportedly eyeing to score a +$1B deal in its next media rights discussion
data.description:
The Ultimate Fighting Championship is looking to secure over $1B per annum from its next media rights deal, more than double what Disney-owned ESPN (NYSE:DIS) is paying the mixed martial arts promotion company at the moment, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

UFC's current agreement with ESPN ends at the end of this year, and the two parties are entering an exclusive negotiating window later this month, the report said. Sources have told Bloomberg that the company had already begun informal conversations.

However, in an emailed statement to Bloomberg, UFC said, “Discussions around our upcoming US domestic rights renewal have not yet begun, and as such we have shared no expectations regarding price. We are in an exclusive negotiating window with ESPN through mid-April 2025 and look forward to productive negotiations when the time comes.”

According to the people familiar with the matter, UFC would like to remain in business with ESPN, which has one deal for regular matches and another for pay-per-view events on its streaming service. Those two deals together pay the UFC about $450M a year.

The report said TKO has talked about dividing its rights between multiple parties and is expected to be the largest ever for the MMA league.

Top TKO executives, CEO Ari Emanuel and president Mark Shapiro, are taking the lead on the negotiations and believe they have at least four or five prospective buyers for the rights, the report said, citing sources.

They include Amazon (AMZN), Netflix (NFLX), Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), as well as Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) YouTube, at least for the pay-per-view matches.

data.title: Texas judge finds American Airlines broke federal law in ESG investing
data.description:
A U.S. district judge in Texas ruled that American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) violated U.S. law governing employee retirement investment management by basing investment decisions on socio-political outcomes rather than exclusively on financial terms.

U.S. District Court Judge for the Northern District of Texas, Reed O’Connor, found in favor of the plaintiffs who sued American Airlines Employee Benefits Committee for mismanaging the retirement plan for American Airlines (NASDAQ:AAL) employees by using BlackRock Institutional Trust Company which pursued “non-financial and nonpecuniary [environmental, social, and governance] policy goals," thereby harming the financial interests of the retirement plan participants.

The decision of the court showed American Airlines Employee Benefits Committee breached certain fiduciary duties under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA).

While the judge ruled that the committee breached its fiduciary duty of loyalty, it did not breach its fiduciary duty of prudence, or conduct, by relying on experts in an area where they are believed to lack expertise.

data.title: IGM Bio plunges as Wall Street downgrades on portfolio shakeup
data.description:
IGM Biosciences (NASDAQ:IGMS) lost nearly two-thirds of its value on Friday after the company abandoned two antibody-based therapeutic candidates and cut roughly 73% of its workforce as part of a portfolio reorganization, triggering several downgrades on Wall Street.

The company has discontinued a bispecific antibody T cell engager called imvotamab, which is targeted at rheumatoid arthritis and systemic lupus erythematosus after its early-stage trial data fell short of expectations. Concurrently, IGMS said it is halting the development of a similar bispecific antibody called IGM-2644.

In reaction, RBC Capital Markets downgraded IGMS to Sector Perform from Outperform. With a price target of $1.50, analyst Brian Abrahams argued that following the announcement, the stock could witness a sharp selloff before settling at its projected cash level in 12 months.

Meanwhile, Stifel downgraded the company to Hold from Buy and set its price target at $2.50, down from $27.00 previously, to reflect $2 per share of net cash expected at the year-end and $0.50 per share value attributed to its ongoing partnership with Sanofi (SNY).

“We believe the ongoing collaboration with SNY—for which IGMS is eligible to receive a collective $3B+ in potential milestone payments—represents an opportunity to leverage the technology platform for difficult-to-treat diseases,” analyst Stephen Willey wrote.

Wedbush, Morgan Stanley, Stifel, and Jefferies were among the other brokerages that downgraded IGMS in reaction to Thursday’s announcement.

data.title: Bitcoin set to fall on weekly basis, slides further from $100K
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is on course to fall 3.5% on a weekly basis, as the selloff intensified in the period.

The cryptocurrency reclaimed its $100K mark on Monday, as it rode the wave of a broad upswing across risk assets. However, BTC could not sustain the climb, and fell away from the threshold.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) fell below the $100K threshold on Tuesday after stronger-than-expected U.S. economic data led investors to further scale back their expectations for interest-rate reductions by the Federal Reserve.

On Thursday, the selloff became stronger and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) along with other high-profile crypto assets slumped such as DOGE and SHIBA INU, as macro jitters mounted and the surge in global bond yields reflecting concerns about upside risks to inflation.

"Market volatility is expected to stay low until NFP on Friday, which should officially kick-start the new trading year with decision makers fully back at work. The highest volatility event for the month is priced to be FOMC at the end of the month as the economic stats are priced to show 'soft landing' signs in the near future," said Augustine Fan, Head of Insights, SOFA.

The demand for Bitcoin is manifesting itself, said Jeff Mei, COO at BTSE, adding, "Now that traders have wrapped up their vacations and are back to work, they've resumed purchases of Bitcoin, crypto, and stocks in a bullish trend as we approach Donald Trump's inauguration.

However, there might be more silver lining, as Bernstein predicted in a recent note that the price of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is set for another triple-digit surge this year, dubbing 2025 the "Infinity Age" of the cryptocurrency space.

"Bitcoin's price is projected to range between $70,000 and $150,000 in 2025, with extreme volatility expected due to market dynamics and geopolitical shifts," SA contributor Florian Grummes said.

Any turmoil in the stock markets will put Bitcoin prices at very high risk as Bitcoin is more closely correlated with US stock markets than ever before, Grummes added.

Other Notable News

  • Cathie Wood, CEO, and CIO of Ark Invest and longtime bitcoin (BTC-USD) advocate, has sold over 36K shares of ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF (ARKB), her bitcoin-backed exchange traded fund, to start off 2025.
  • HIVE Digital Technologies (HIVE) said it mined 103 Bitcoins (BTC-USD) in December, the same as last month.
  • Hut 8 (HUT) saw Wall Street analysts reiterate their bullish stance on the vertically integrated operator of energy infrastructure and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) miners after securing approval for a data center project.
  • MicroStrategy's (MSTR) weekly bitcoin buying binge continued as it added another $101M worth of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) during the two days ended 2024
  • Riot Platforms (RIOT) produced 516 bitcoins in December, up 4% from 495 Bitcoins produced in November, but down 17% from 619 bitcoins produced in December 2023.
  • Strive Asset Management, co-founded by Trump ally Vivek Ramaswamy, has filed for a Bitcoin (BTC-USD) bond ETF focused on convertible securities issued by MicroStrategy (MSTR) or other companies that will invest all or most of the proceeds to buy the cryptocurrency.

Moves of other cryptocurrencies

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose 1.6% to $94.8K as at 2:19 pm. Ether (ETH-USD) was up 0.3% to $3.3K, while DOGE (DOGE-USD) up 2.9% to $0.33.

data.title: Meta ends DEI programs in latest sweeping change ahead of Trump's second term
data.description:
Meta (NASDAQ:META) is ending its major diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI) programs, effective immediately, Axios reported Friday, citing an internal memo intended for company employees.

The move is among many sweeping changes the Mark Zuckerberg-led company has made within this week ahead of Donald Trump's second term as U.S. president.

Meta this week ended its third-party fact-checking program and adopted "Community Notes," akin to Elon Musk's X.

By adopting policies similar to the platform owned by the second-biggest Trump donor, Zuckerberg appears to be trying to get on the better side of the incoming president, who historically has been critical of the Facebook founder.

The company also appointed UFC CEO Dana White to the board of directors earlier this week, a prolific Trump campaigner.

Find the full memo from Meta below, which was made public by Axios:
Hi all, I wanted to share some changes we're making to our hiring, development and procurement practices. Before getting into the details, there is some important background to lay out:The legal and policy landscape surrounding diversity, equity and inclusion efforts in the United States is changing. The Supreme Court of the United States has recently made decisions signaling a shift in how courts will approach DEI. It reaffirms longstanding principles that discrimination should not be tolerated or promoted on the basis of inherent characteristics. The term "DEI" has also become charged, in part because it is understood by some as a practice that suggests preferential treatment of some groups over others.At Meta, we have a principle of serving everyone. This can be achieved through cognitively diverse teams, with differences in knowledge, skills, political views, backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. Such teams are better at innovating, solving complex problems and identifying new opportunities which ultimately helps us deliver on our ambition to build products that serve everyone. On top of that, we've always believed that no-one should be given - or deprived- of opportunities because of protected characteristics, and that has not changed.Given the shifting legal and policy landscape, we're making the following changes:
  • On hiring, we will continue to source candidates from different backgrounds, but we will stop using the Diverse Slate Approach. This practice has always been subject to public debate and is currently being challenged. We believe there are other ways to build an industry-leading workforce and leverage teams made up of world-class people from all types of backgrounds to build products that work for everyone.
  • We previously ended representation goals for women and ethnic minorities. Having goals can create the impression that decisions are being made based on race or gender. While this has never been our practice, we want to eliminate any impression of it.
  • We are sunsetting our supplier diversity efforts within our broader supplier strategy. This effort focused on sourcing from diverse-owned businesses; going forward, we will focus our efforts on supporting small and medium sized businesses that power much of our economy. Opportunities will continue to be available to all qualified suppliers, including those who were part of the supplier diversity program.
  • Instead of equity and inclusion training programs, we will build programs that focus on how to apply fair and consistent practices that mitigate bias for all, no matter your background.
  • We will no longer have a team focused on DEI. Maxine Williams is taking on a new role at Meta, focused on accessibility and engagement.
What remains the same are the principles we've used to guide our People practices:
  1. We serve everyone. We are committed to making our products accessible, beneficial and universally impactful for everyone.
  2. We build the best teams with the most talented people. This means sourcing people from a range of candidate pools, but never making hiring decisions based on protected characteristics (e.g. race, gender etc.). We will always evaluate people as individuals.
  3. We drive consistency in employment practices to ensure fairness and objectivity for all. We do not provide preferential treatment, extra opportunities or unjustified credit to anyone based on protected characteristics nor will we devalue impact based on these characteristics.
  4. We build connection and community. We support our employee communities, people who use our products, and those in the communities where we operate. Our employee community groups (MRGs) continue to be open to all.
Meta has the privilege to serve billions of people every day. It's important to us that our products are accessible to all, and are useful in promoting economic growth and opportunity around the world. We continue to be focused on serving everyone, and building a multi-talented, industry-leading workforce from all walks of life.
